decision-tree.root-question: Can a multi-stakeholder 30-day trial actually run for this purchase?

branch.when: candidate_count >= 2 AND trial_sandbox_feasible == true AND stakeholders_available >= 2
branch.question: Is the rollback gate (criteria + responsible owner + day-60 switch path) defined BEFORE the trial begins?

branch.conclusion: Run the 30-day trial against real workloads; score each axis with the relevant stakeholder; lock decision after r5-revisit-annually-aligned signature.
Block. Define rollback (criteria + owner) before starting the trial — without it, lock-in dominates the decision.
Skip the rubric — single-vendor markets do not benefit from scoring. Document the single-vendor situation and revisit annually.
Escalate to procurement / legal — without a trial sandbox the 30-day rule cannot hold; selection requires a different process (RFP).
Defer until at least 2 stakeholders (eng + ops or eng + finance) can score. Single-scorer rubrics encode that scorer's bias as decision.

example.p: 4 candidates, trial sandbox available, eng+ops+finance ready, rollback gate "if MTTR > baseline by day 60 switch back" defined. → conclusion r2-trial-30-day-min → run trial.
